messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what limitation is mentioned for ARP?

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what limitation is mentioned for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: In a VLAN with Virtual Active Gateway and DHCP relay, hosts will be unable to resolve DHCP IP received over a peer DHCP relay on a L2VNI. Scenario: DHCP offer received over an L2VNI that is relayed by a VTEP peer will not be processed and gets dropped. Workaround: Configure unique source IP (Loopback IP) for DHCP relay agent that gets the DHCP offer on a L3VNI to the originating relay agent and disable dhcp relay for l2vpn-clients.

description: Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in "device-mode" instead of "client-mode":{color:#000000}interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device{color}

description: Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal: 6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

description: Symptom: Missing ARP entries showing as "incomplete" in the ARP table, causing connectivity issues in remote VTEP. Scenario: This issue may occur in an EVPN VXLAN environment with VSX pairs when a VIP (Virtual IP) moves from one node to another node, MAC address aging occurs or after executing "clear mac-address" on the switch. Workaround: A temporary workaround is to add a static ARP entry for the affected IP addresses.

description: Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with the "snmp-server response-source" command prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file/fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing[snmp] srcipaddrentry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

description: Symptom: Phyport is missing on a remote VTEP ARP table when a shut/no shut is performed on interfaces of the local host connected in local VTEP VSX pairs. Workaround: Clear ARP on the local VTEP.

description: Symptom: In an L2VPN network, when a DHCP client sends an IP request with the broadcast flag unset (unicast), duplicate DHCP client packets are observed at the DHCP server-one from each VTEP. As a result, the client does not receive an IP address. Scenario: To prevent duplicate DHCP client packets from each VTEP in an L2VPN topology, it is recommended to configure "no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients" on the switch. However, after saving the switch configuration and rebooting, the relay daemon does not correctly apply all configuration settings at boot time, even though the configuration persists in the database. This causes the duplicate packet issue to reappear. Workaround: To restore correct behavior, manually enable and then disable the L2VPN client configuration using "dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients" followed by "no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients".

description: Symptom: In a VLAN with Virtual Active Gateway and DHCP relay, hosts will be unable to resolve DHCP IP received over a peer DHCP relay on a L2VNI. Scenario: This occurs when a DHCP offer is received over an L2VNI that is relayed by a VTEP peer and is processed and gets dropped. Workaround: Configure unique source IP (Loopback IP) for DHCP relay agent that gets the DHCP offer on a L3VNI to the originating relay agent and disable dhcp relay for l2vpn-clients.

description: Symptom: On 8325P Switch series, PTP may not working after reboot of a PTP-unaware neighbor device on deployments with a large number of PTP clients (over 20 per port). Scenario: This issue will only be seen if the default PTP timers are modified and the device has a large number of PTP clients configured (over 20 per port). Workaround: For large scale PTP deployments it is recommended to keep the PTP timers set to their defaults.

description: Symptom: On 8325P Switch series, PTP may not working after reboot of a PTP-unaware neighbor device on deployments with a large number of PTP clients (over 20 per port). Scenario: This issue will only be seen if the default PTP timers are modified and the device has a large number of PTP clients configured (over 20 per port). Workaround: For large scale PTP deployments it is recommended to keep the PTP timers set to their defaults.

description: Symptom: Flood and Learn doesn't work for random VNIs on 8325 and 8325P Switch series. Scenario: This issue can occur during a VSX upgrade with VXLAN replication mode set to underlay-multicast. Workaround: Reconfigure the impacted VNIs to restore back the flood and learn.

description: Symptom: Traffic loss occurs for IPv4 /32 or IPv6 /128 host-routes with matching directly connected hosts during certain network events. Scenario: This issue is most often seen when the switch reboots, but it may also occur when it initially starts up. Link-flaps could also trigger this issue. Workaround: Clear the ARP cache.
